channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: By the Numbers: Why trick-or-treaters may bag more gummy candy than chocolate this Halloween
item.description: Ghouls, goblins … and gummy bears. Trick-or-treaters may find more fruity candy than chocolate among their Halloween handouts this year.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Historic libraries bring modern comfort to book lovers and history buffs in New England
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — When David Arsenault takes down a worn, leather-bound 19th-century book from the winding shelves of the Boston Athenaeum, he feels a sense of awe — like he’s handling an artifact in a museum.
item.dc:creator: LEAH WILLINGHAM and KIMBERLEE KRUESI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: What is 'ghost tapping?' New scheme targeting tap-to-pay users
item.description: The Better Business Bureau is warning tap-to-pay users of a new scheme that could make simple transactions costly.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Electric heat to cost more this winter, federal agency projects
item.description: The U.S. Energy Information Administration said average U.S. consumers who rely on electricity will see expenditures rise 4%.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Ontario premier says he'll pull ad that upset Trump so trade talks between Canada and US can resume
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — The leader of Canada’s most populous province said Friday he’ll pull the anti-tariff ad that prompted U.S. President Donald Trump to end trade talks with Canada.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 5 shot near Howard University homecoming week; 2 people in custody
item.description: Five people were hospitalized after a shooting near Howard University Friday, according to the DC Fire and EMS Department.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlyn Dillon, NewsNation

item.title: Inspector Clouseau? The mystery man in an AP photo after the Louvre jewel heist creates a buzz
item.description: PARIS (AP) — It was shortly after the stunning heist of the crown jewels at the Louvre when Paris-based Associated Press photographer Thibault Camus caught in his frame a dapperly dressed young man walking by uniformed French police officers, their car blocking one of the museum gates.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Shots fired, driver hurt and held after truck rams into Coast Guard base in California
item.description: A truck driver who backed into a U.S. Coast Guard base in the San Francisco Bay Area — the site of earlier protests against federal immigration agents — was shot by law enforcement officers and wounded, authorities said Friday.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 99-year-old WWII veteran shares culinary talents
item.description: Every week, Vincent Martinez can be found cooking for others, a passion that traces back to his time in the Navy during World War II.
item.dc:creator: Redrick Terry, NewsNation

item.title: Walmart is changing how hourly store workers earn raises
item.description: The retail giant's new approach will take employee performance into account as well as years of service.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Here's what the White House lost with the East Wing's demolition
item.description: The East Wing's razing was met with criticism both by preservationists and by over half of the American public.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Task force says man to be deported 3rd time after arrest
item.description: The task force said he has an active order of removal from the country and will be deported for the third time.
item.dc:creator: David Royer, NewsNation

item.title: In emotional speech, Zohran Mamdani defends Muslim identity against 'racist and baseless' attacks
item.description: Zohran Mamdani, the Democratic nominee for New York City mayor, is pledging to embrace his Muslim identity amid growing attacks in the final days of the campaign.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Cholesterol medication recalled over 'failed dissolution specifications': What to know
item.description: “In some instances, stopping your medicine may be more harmful to your health than continuing to take the recalled medicine,” the FDA says.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Trump speaks at tele-rally to boost GOP's chances in governor's race in New Jersey
item.description: President Donald Trump is urging his supporters in New Jersey to vote for Republican gubernatorial candidate Jack Ciattarelli.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mafia returns to center stage in NBA betting scandal that included rigged poker games
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Decades after a crackdown by prosecutors decimated the ranks of the New York Mafia, the indictment of an NBA coach, a player and nearly three dozen others in a betting scandal highlighted the mob's persistence and adaptability to changing times and technology.
item.dc:creator: ADAM GELLER and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Thousands of curling irons recalled amid burn injuries
item.description: Thousands of curling irons sold at popular retailers, including on Amazon, are being recalled after reports of the curling iron barrel detaching, posing a burn risk.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, Fox 5

item.title: 3 convicted for illegal BASE jumping in Yosemite National Park
item.description: “We do not tolerate illegal activity in Yosemite National Park,” Yosemite National Park Superintendent Raymond McPadden said.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, NewsNation

item.title: Cholesterol medication recalled over 'failed dissolution specifications': What to know
item.description: Over 141,000 bottles of cholesterol medication have been recalled due to “failed dissolution specifications,” suggesting that the drugs did not pass a solubility test, or failed to dissolve at a standard rate.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Target is eliminating 1,800 corporate jobs as it looks to reclaim its lost luster
item.description: Target said Thursday that it is eliminating about 1,800 corporate positions in an effort to streamline decision-making and accelerate initiatives to rebuild the flagging discount retailer’s customer base.
item.dc:creator: LISA LEFF, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Dashcam video shows deadly freeway crash involving suspected illegal immigrant trucker
item.description: Federal officials warned California about truck license compliance issues before deadly freeway crash involving suspected illegal immigrant driver killed three.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Social Security recipients get a 2.8% cost-of-living boost in 2026
item.description: The Social Security cost-of-living increase will go into effect for Social Security recipients beginning in January.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man shot 10 times protecting high school cheerleader who died after shooting at bonfire: report
item.description: An Alabama man was shot 10 times while trying to protect a high school cheerleader at a bonfire on early Sunday, according to a report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alaska Airlines resumes operations after an IT outage grounded its flights for hours
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — Alaska Airlines said its operations have resumed Friday after it had to ground its planes for hours because of an information technology outage.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Las Vegas connections to illegal gambling investigation, NBA fixing
item.description: An arrest was made in Las Vegas Thursday morning in connection with the FBI investigation of illegal gambling involving current and former NBA coaches and players.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Where the jewels stolen from the Louvre Museum might end up
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Just days after a stunning heist at the Louvre Museum in Paris, speculation is growing around where the lavish, stolen jewels that once adorned France's royals might end up.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and R.J. RICO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bodycam footage of Jay Cutler's arrest released
item.description: Cutler, 41, was arrested in October 2024 after police responded to a minor traffic collision where he had rear-ended another vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, KRON4

item.title: Medicare agency recalling thousands of furloughed staffers for open enrollment
item.description: The Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) is temporarily calling back all furloughed staff starting next week, a spokesperson confirmed Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KRON4

item.title: Transportation Secretary Duffy ‘can’t guarantee’ on-time flights during shutdown
item.description: Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy warned Thursday of possible flight delays and cancellations during the government shutdown, saying the federal safety system depends on air traffic controllers working without pay. “I can’t guarantee you that your flight’s going to be on time.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he's owed a 'lot of money' over federal probes. Here's how the government could pay him
item.description: The potential that the president might take taxpayer money from the same government he leads has raised numerous ethical questions, especially since Trump has made cutting federal spending a top administration priority.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Amazon hopes robots can replace 600K future hires: Report
item.description: Amazon hopes robots can replace hundreds of thousands of future roles, according to leaked documents seen by The New York Times.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mamdani, Cuomo and Sliwa clash bitterly during final NYC mayoral debate
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Zohran Mamdani was attacked over his thin resume, Republican Curtis Sliwa strove to prove his seriousness as a candidate and former Gov.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A timeline of US attacks off South America and what Congress has had to say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In less than two months, President Donald Trump and Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth say the U.S. military has killed at least 37 people in nine strikes against drug-smuggling vessels in the Caribbean Sea and eastern Pacific Ocean.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and KONSTANTIN TOROPIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pentagon touts 'next generation' press corps of mostly right-wing outlets
item.description: The Defense Department on Wednesday announced the "next generation" of the Pentagon press corps, including mostly right-wing outlets, following the mass exodus of legacy outlets from the building who refused to sign the department’s restrictive new press policy.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Minnesota court gives win to transgender powerlifter who was barred from competing as a woman
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — The Minnesota Supreme Court on Wednesday delivered a win to a transgender weightlifter, saying she's protected by the state's expansive human rights law, while the organization that banned her from competing in its women's division said it still has a chance.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI and MARGERY A. BECK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Latest: North Carolina lawmakers approve map adding a GOP House seat
item.description: North Carolina lawmakers gave final approval Wednesday to a revised U.S. House map backed by President Donald Trump that is intended to help Republicans win an additional seat in next year’s elections. The new congressional map reshapes the state’s only current swing district, held by Democratic U.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: A dozen UN international staffers leave Yemen after being released by Houthis
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Houthi rebels released a dozen United Nations international staffers Wednesday and allowed three others to move freely within the U.N. compound after detaining them in the facility in Yemen's capital over the weekend, according to the world body.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, ABC 4
